audit_items.proof_trace: permutateAlt() allocates `state = new size_t[L]` at L79, but the early return at L84 (`if (in[i].size() == 0) return {}`) exits without calling `delete[] state`, causing a heap memory leak. The normal exit path at L129-130 correctly calls `delete[] state`, but this guard is bypassed on the early return. No RAII wrapper or exception-safe mechanism protects the allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker supplies crafted CSS input containing a parent-selector reference that resolves to an empty selector group; permutateAlt is called from resolve_parent_refs (ast_selectors.cpp:980) and the early return at L84 fires, leaking the `state` array on each invocation — repeated requests accumulate unbounded heap allocations leading to memory exhaustion (DoS).
